overview: Tron is a Modern archetype focused on ramping mana by assembling Urza's Mine, Urza's Tower, and Urza's Power Plant to generate seven mana by turn three. Once the combo is assembled, the deck wins by casting gigantic creatures, using taxing artifacts, and controlling the board with versatile removal.
win_conditions: Cast gigantic creatures like Emrakul, the World Anew
Use Kozilek's Command for board control and removal
Attack with an indestructible The One Ring
Fetch finishers like Cityscape Leveler or Sundering Titan with Karn

keep_criteria: Having three Tron pieces already in hand
Having two Tron pieces and an Expedition Map or Devourer of Destiny
Having a Tron piece, an Expedition Map, and a Devourer of Destiny
Hands with Ugin's Labyrinth and Mind Stone if you have a 4-drop
Keep hands with two or more Tron lands (Urza’s Mine, Power Plant, Tower) plus a cantrip or acceleration spell.
Keep hands with one Tron land and two non-Tron lands if they include a cantrip and a threat.
mulligan_criteria: Aggressively mulligan to maximize the number of Tron pieces in hand
Mulligan if you lack the consistency pieces to assemble Tron by turn 3
Mulligan on three or more non-Tron lands, or hands with only one land and no Tron pieces or cantrips.

examples.reason: Having three Tron pieces already in hand guarantees a turn 3 combo.
Having a Tron piece, an Expedition Map, and a Devourer of Destiny is a typical keep.

early_game: Dedicate turns 1 and 2 to assembling three different Tron lands
Cast Expedition Map on turn 1 if holding two Tron pieces to crack on turn 2
Use Ugin's Labyrinth to enable a turn 1 Mind Stone for a turn 2 4-drop
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a cantrip (e.g., Thoughtseize or Into the Void) to dig for Tron pieces or strip key interaction.
Turn 2: Play a Tron land or cast a cantrip to assemble Tron
if Tron assembles, cast Karn or Ugin.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast your turn-2 or turn-3 threat while maintaining mana for protection.
mid_game: Cast gigantic creatures and expensive colorless cards once Tron is assembled
Use Kozilek's Command for removal, card draw, or graveyard exile
Deploy taxing artifacts like Chalice of the Void or The One Ring
Prioritize assembling Tron over playing basic or utility lands.
Use cantrips aggressively to dig for Tron pieces and maintain card advantage.
Manage your graveyard by exiling opponent threats with Karn or Ugin, and avoid giving free targets to reanimation or storm strategies.
Commit Karn early to protect your board, exile threats, and draw cards.
Hold Ugin until you have a clear board state or need to wipe a stabilized opponent.
late_game: Maintain board control with All Is Dust or Kozilek's Command
Use Karn, the Great Creator to tutor sideboard artifacts or finishers
Reset The One Ring to avoid life tax or attack with it
Karn (exiles threats, draws cards, protects your board), Ugin (board wipe, card advantage, removes problematic permanents), and Emrakul (game-winning threat).
Use Karn to exile opponent win conditions and draw into your own finishers.
Go all-in when you have Tron online, a Karn or Ugin in play, and enough mana to cast a finisher while holding interaction.
Commit when the opponent has no clear path to stabilize or when you can safely attack through their remaining resources.

key_cards.name: Urza's Mine
key_cards.notes: Part of the three-piece land combo to generate 7 mana.

key_cards.name: Urza's Tower
key_cards.notes: Provides extra mana when played as a second copy after assembling the combo.

key_cards.name: Expedition Map
key_cards.notes: Critical for assembling perfect Tron on turn 3; cast turn 1, crack turn 2.

key_cards.name: Devourer of Destiny
key_cards.notes: Filters draws for Tron pieces and exiles colored permanents.

key_cards.name: Emrakul, the World Anew
key_cards.notes: Protected on cast, takes control of opponent's creatures, can be cast for 6 mana via madness.

key_cards.name: Karn, the Great Creator
key_cards.notes: Fetches artifacts from exile or sideboard, creates indestructible tokens.

key_cards.name: The One Ring
key_cards.notes: Indestructible 4/4 attacker that taxes life; can be reset by playing another copy.

key_cards.name: Mind Stone
key_cards.notes: Enables turn 2 plays like Expedition Map or 4-drops when paired with Ugin's Labyrinth.

key_cards.name: All Is Dust
key_cards.notes: Staple colorless removal that exiles all nonbasic lands and creatures.

key_cards.name: Chalice of the Void
key_cards.notes: Essential against cheap spell decks like Burn or Cascade.

sequencing_tips: Play a second Urza's Tower after assembling the combo for extra mana.
Cast Expedition Map on turn 1 with two Tron pieces to crack on turn 2.
Discard Emrakul, the World Anew with The Underworld Cookbook to cast it for 6 mana via madness.
Reset The One Ring by playing another copy to avoid the life tax or maintain an indestructible attacker.
Use Karn, the Great Creator to fetch artifacts exiled by Devourer of Destiny or from the opponent's graveyard.
Tron is highly dependent on land draws
use cantrips aggressively and consider sideboard land destruction to smooth your draws.
keep hands with at least two lands.
Force of Negation and Eye of the Storms are niche but powerful in specific matchups, so use them to protect key spells or disrupt combo decks.
Don’t forget to use Karn’s -3 to exile opponent graveyard threats, and save Ugin’s -3 for when you need a board wipe but can’t afford a sweeper.

matchups.advice: Side in Chalice of the Void to tax cheap spells.
Side in Chalice of the Void to counter cheap combo pieces.
Side in Tormod's Crypt to disrupt graveyard recursion.
Side in Grafdigger's Cage and Pithing Needle to shut down activated abilities.
Side in The Stone Brain to exile key lands.
Side in Liquimetal Coating to destroy lands by turning them into artifacts.
Side out All Is Dust and side in The Stone Brain or The Filigree Sylex.
Be cautious; the deck is vulnerable to nonbasic land hate.
Use Force of Negation to protect key spells. Cast threats in response to counterspells to force them to spend resources. Use Karn to exile countered spells or draw into answers.
Use Ghost Quarter or land destruction to disrupt their mana. Play fast threats before they can stabilize. Use Ugin to wipe their board and regain advantage.
Use Dispel or Abrade to remove early threats. Play Karn to stabilize and draw cards. Don’t overextend; trade efficiently and use your late-game threats to close out the game.

Pressure with early threats to force them to use resources. Use Karn to dig through their deck and find answers. Force them to interact with your threats while you assemble Tron and deploy game-winners.
Win the resource war. Use Thoughtseize to strip their Tron pieces and key threats. Use Karn to exile their threats and draw cards. Prioritize graveyard hate and discard to out-value them.

common_threats: Land destruction and nonbasic land hate
Watch for discard spells, counterspells, and board wipes.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: each of them has two key components to win games: taxing your opponents, and ramping your mana so you can play your big spells as early as turn 3 thanks to a natural Tron.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: After that, you want to cast your gigantic creatures and use Kozilek's Commander to control the game a bit.
When all else fails, you can always swing with a 4/4 indestructible The One Ring
Cityscape Leveler and Sundering Titan : Fetch these when you need a finisher if you don’t already have a big creature in hand.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: A second Urza's Tower is better than your second copies of the other Tron pieces once you’ve assembled the combo, as it provides an extra mana.
the plan being to cast it on turn 1 if you have at least two different Tron pieces in hand. This way, you can crack it on turn 2
You can discard Emrakul, the World Anew with The Underworld Cookbook , and still cast it using its madness ability for a reduced cost of 6 mana.
Remember that you can “reset” your The One Ring by simply putting another copy of it on the field.
Thanks to Karn, the Great Creator ‘s ability, you can fetch any artifact that was incidentally exiled with Devourer of Destiny

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: The most common strategy for beating a Tron deck in Modern is land destruction or nonbasic land hate
Rakdos Midrange decks running Fulminator Mage in the sideboard
Hand hate in the form of Thoughtseize is key at removing your opponents’ most significant threats
the most powerful ones being Blood Moon and Harbinger of the Seas
useful to have if you run up against Boseiju, Who Endures , Assassin's Trophy , or Demolition Field
handle early threats like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer
